周忌: death anniversary; anniversary of a person's death
壱岐: Iki (former province located on Iki Island in present-day Nagasaki Prefecture)
於いて: at (a time or place); in; on
飢える: to starve; to be famished; to be hungry
整頓: orderliness; put in order; tidying up
累計: cumulative total; accumulated total; total up to now
落ち鮎: sweetfish going downstream to spawn (in autumn)
浄瑠璃: jōruri; type of dramatic recitation accompanied by a shamisen (associated with Japanese puppet theater)
襟足: hairline at the nape of the neck; nape
初詣: one's first visit to a temple [shrine] in the new year
煩悩: worldly desires; evil passions; appetites of the flesh
渇く: to be thirsty; to feel thirsty
窮める: carry to extremity, reach an extreme
老朽: infirmity (due to old age); decrepitude; senility
霜降り: speckled with white; salt-and-pepper (pattern, fabric, etc.)
睦月: first month of the lunar calendar
嗣ぐ: to succeed (a person, to a position, etc.), to inherit, to take over
馴れ初め: start of a romance; beginning of love
萎む: to wither (of flowers, dreams, etc.); to wilt; to droop; to shrivel; to fade (away); to sag; to deflate
苛める: to ill-treat; to bully; to torment
苛立つ: to be irritated; to get annoyed; to lose one's patience
勃起: erection (of the penis); becoming erect; stiffening
奔走する: bustle about, exert oneself for, devote oneself to
拐う: to carry off; to run away with; to sweep away; to snatch; to kidnap; to abduct
手酌: helping oneself to a drink; pouring one's own drink
濫用: abuse; misuse; misappropriation; using to excess
這い出る: to crawl out of; to creep out of
臆する: to be hesitant; to feel timid
天皇陛下: His Majesty the Emperor, His Imperial Majesty
眉唾: dubious tale; cock-and-bull story; questionable information
頒つ: to divide, to separate, to share
公卿: high-ranking court noble; senior court official; kugyō
総帥: commander-in-chief; leader; head of a group of companies
殉ずる: to sacrifice oneself; to die a martyr
更迭する: reshuffle, change (as of government officials), exchange places
和洋折衷: mixing of Japanese and Western elements
丙午: Fire Horse (43rd term of the sexagenary cycle, e.g. 1906, 1966, 2026)
隼人: Hayato; ethnic group which lived in Kyūshū in ancient times and opposed the state of Yamato
俳諧: haikai (collective name for haiku, haibun, haiga, senryū, etc.)
梓弓: catalpa bow; spirit bow; bow used to summon spirits, made of Japanese cherry birch, catalpa, or other wood
楷書: block [square] style used in writing or printing Chinese characters
拝謁: audience with someone of high rank (e.g. the emperor)
澪つくし: marks in a water channel, channel buoys
重禁錮: major imprisonment, imprisonment with hard labor (hard labour)
凌ぐ: to endure; to keep out (e.g. rain); to stave off (e.g. starvation)
三つ巴: circular pattern of three commas; three-sided fight
蒔絵: gold or silver lacquer; lacquer decoration sprinkled with metal powder
坐像: seated figure (e.g. of Buddha); sedentary statue; sedentary image
皐月: fifth month of the lunar calendar, satsuki azalea (Rhododendron indicum)
末梢: tip, end; tip of a twig
蒲焼: kabayaki; loach or eel dipped and broiled in soy-based sauce
惣菜: small dish (served as a part of an ordinary household meal); side dish
獲麟: the end of things (esp. used for one's last writings), one's dying hour (esp. used for the death of Confucius)
諒する: to acknowledge, to understand, to appreciate
嵩む: to mount up; to pile up; to accumulate; to increase
檀家: family which supports a temple; parishioner
稜線: ridgeline; lines of a mountain ridge
茅葺き: thatching a roof (with grass); thatched roof
五穀豊穣: huge harvest (of the five grains), bumper crop, abundant crop
に亘り: throughout, over a period of ..., over a span of ...
庭燎: garden bonfire (esp. one held on the imperial grounds)
錯綜: becoming complicated, becoming involved, being intricate
水茄子: var. of eggplant (Solanum melongena) which can be eaten raw
黎明: daybreak; dawn; gray of the morning (grey)
黎明期: dawn (of a new era, civilization, etc.); dawning
偲ぶ: to recollect; to remember; to reminisce
亦亦: once again, yet again, (there you go) again
蕉風: correct style in a haiku (like Basho's)
勁草: wind-resistant blade of grass; resistant idea (metaphorically)
晨鴨: harlequin duck (Clangula hyemalis or Histrionicus histrionicus)
花一匁: children's game in which two groups compete for members by playing janken
束脩: entrance fee, initiation fee, registration fee
侃侃諤諤: outspoken, arguing (heatedly) what one believes in, insisting on something without restraint
文質彬彬: harmony of beauty and substance, (perfect) balance of looks and character, harmony of refinement and simplicity
